claims.claim: Digipower (Taiwan) is identified as a key vendor in the global data center Rack PDU market by market research institutions, combining OEM/ODM design expertise with quality assurance for global OEM partners

By combining OEM/ODM design expertise with rigorous quality assurance, Digipower continues to advance data center innovations.

claims.excerpt: The Europe data center PDUs and PSUs market is projected to reach $20,056.7 million by 2035 from $2,455.7 million in 2024, growing at a CAGR of 21.23% during the forecast period 2025-2035.

claims.excerpt: the data center PDU market size was valued USD 3.94 biliion in 2025 and is project to reach USD 14.23 billion by 2032, growing at a CAGR of 19.5% from 2026 to 2032.

claims.claim: Estonian colocation provider Greenergy deploys Vertiv rack PDUs via A-Kaabel, an authorized Vertiv sales/service partner since 2020, rather than white-label ODM PDUs
claims.sourceTitle: Greenergy and A-Kaabel deploy Vertiv™ rack PDUs to power scalable data center growth in Estonia
claims.excerpt: Since 2020, A-Kaabel has been an authorized sales and service partner for Vertiv in Estonia.

claims.claim: A Serbian telecom integrator (SAGA) selected Vertiv monitored PDUs based on fast delivery and DCIM compatibility rather than unit cost
claims.sourceTitle: SAGA and Vertiv Integrate Racks and Monitored PDUs for Serbian Telecom Leader A Vertiv Case Study
claims.excerpt: a leading Serbian telecom company was searching for a server rack solution with fast delivery and monitored PDUs (power distribution units) compatible with their existing DCIM (Data Center Infrastructure Management) solution.

claims.claim: A regional integrator (Technosector) at MIND Park in Serbia specified Legrand Data Center Solutions rather than an ODM/white-label alternative
claims.excerpt: Legrand Data Center Solutions technology has been specified by Technosector, a key partner for the site's owner, MIND Park.

claims.claim: Schleifenbauer developed its PDU 5.0 product based on input from over 40 European data centre managers, citing licence-free monitoring and customization as key demands, not lowest price
claims.sourceTitle: Case study PDU 5.0 en EnerTree DCEM Software
claims.excerpt: The Schleifenbauer PDU 5.0 wasn't conceived in a lab, but shaped through conversations with over 40 data centre managers across Europe.

claims.claim: Schleifenbauer partners with DPI (Datalec Precision Installations) as an official installation partner for server rack PDUs in UK data centres, emphasizing customization and short lead times over price
claims.sourceTitle: Schleifenbauer & DPI: Reliable Rack PDUs for UK Data Centres
claims.excerpt: DPI needed a PDU partner that could provide reliable, tailored solutions without long lead times—ensuring seamless integration and future-proof performance.

claims.claim: A European bank's PDU vendor migration case study emphasizes unified management platform and phased transition, not price competition among OEM/white-label suppliers
claims.sourceTitle: Data Centre PDU Migration & Rack Security | European Bank Case Study
claims.excerpt: The solution had to support both legacy and new PDUs simultaneously, while keeping power monitoring, environmental data, and security workflows under one roof.

tensions.text: cust.oem estimates OEM/integrator white-label channel at 0.22 of PDU spend, but every integrator case study found (Vertiv/Greenergy, Vertiv/SAGA, Legrand/MIND Park, Schleifenbauer/DPI) shows integrators selecting branded incumbent vendors on features, certification, DCIM compatibility, and support—not the Taiwanese/Chinese ODM white-label suppliers (Digipower, Geteknet) the node's rationale assumes are contestable.

No source in this group quantifies the fraction of PDU spend flowing through OEM/integrator white-label channels; all market-size figures (Europe $2.46B→$20.06B, global $3.94B→$14.23B) are undifferentiated by channel or buyer type, leaving the 0.22 ratio in cust.oem without any corroborating breakdown.

absences: No channel-share study or distributor/operator interview data was found quantifying what fraction of PDU spend flows through OEM/integrator white-label channels versus branded vendors.
No data distinguishing Taiwanese/Chinese ODM (Digipower, Geteknet) supplied volume/value from regional branded vendor (Panduit, Schneider, Rittal) volume within the OEM/integrator channel in Europe.

No pricing or bid-award data showing whether integrator procurement decisions in Central Europe are won on unit cost versus features/certification/local support at a quantified rate — only qualitative case studies were found, all favoring branded vendors.
No estimate of what portion of the contestable OEM/integrator channel a regional manufacturer could realistically capture.
